name: Endotracheal intubation
description: Endotracheal intubation by any route or technique (oral/nasal, fibreoptic, awake, emergency, double-lumen); surgical airways excluded.

en.name: Endotracheal intubation
en.shortDescription: Endotracheal intubation by any route or technique (oral/nasal, fibreoptic, awake, emergency, double-lumen); surgical airways excluded.
en.longDescription:
## Definition & Clinical Context

Endotracheal intubation is the act of placing a tube through the mouth or nose, past the larynx, into the trachea to secure the airway for mechanical ventilation, anaesthesia, or airway protection. In source data this is mostly a **procedure** event marking when a patient was intubated, recorded in intensive care, anaesthesia, and emergency settings. The set also keeps two non-procedure qualifiers about the intubation itself — whether it was unplanned and whether it was difficult (see Included Concepts). There is no unit. The various concepts differ by route (oral vs nasal), technique (direct laryngoscopy, fibreoptic, rigid bronchoscope, retrograde, digital, via a laryngeal mask), and circumstance (awake, emergency).

## Included Concepts

The procedures are anchored on the SNOMED concept *Intubation of respiratory tract* with its descendants, then the surgical-airway branch is removed (tracheostomy, cricothyroidotomy and related neck-stoma procedures live in a separate set — see Excluded Concepts). Two LOINC qualifier concepts are added explicitly.

### Generic intubation (default)

- *40487536 / 447996002 — Intubation of respiratory tract (SNOMED)*: the broadest intubation concept; the default when the source does not specify route or technique.
- *4013354 / 112798008 — Insertion of endotracheal tube (SNOMED)*: insertion of an endotracheal tube (the core endotracheal intubation concept).

### By route

- *4335481 / 232674004 — Orotracheal intubation (SNOMED)*: via the mouth.
- *4337616 / 232679009 — Nasotracheal intubation (SNOMED)*: via the nose.
- *4339623 / 232675003 — Oral intubation awake (SNOMED)*: oral, patient awake.
- *4335584 / 232680007 — Nasal intubation awake (SNOMED)*: nasal, patient awake.
- *4337045 / 232681006 — Blind nasal intubation (SNOMED)*: nasal, without direct visualisation.

### By technique

- *4179373 / 429161001 — Insertion of endotracheal tube using laryngoscope (SNOMED)*: direct laryngoscopy.
- *4337615 / 232678001 — Orotracheal fiberoptic intubation (SNOMED)* and *4337617 / 232682004 — Nasotracheal fiberoptic intubation (SNOMED)*: flexible fibreoptic-guided.
- *4335583 / 232677006 — Tracheal intubation using rigid bronchoscope (SNOMED)*: via a rigid bronchoscope.
- *4287921 / 397892004 — Retrograde intubation (SNOMED)*: tube placed over a retrograde guidewire.
- *4140765 / 427331009 — Digital assisted intubation (SNOMED)*: guided by the operator's fingers.
- *4303945 / 418613003 — Tracheal intubation through a laryngeal mask airway (SNOMED)*: tube passed through an in-situ laryngeal mask.

### By circumstance

- *4119642 / 304341005 — Awake intubation (SNOMED)*: performed with the patient awake.
- *4058031 / 16883004 — Endotracheal intubation, emergency procedure (SNOMED)*: emergency intubation.
- *4082243 / 182682004 — Emergency laryngeal intubation (SNOMED)*: emergency laryngeal intubation.
- *4283807 / 66861000 — Intubation of larynx (SNOMED)*: laryngeal intubation (general).
- *4056812 / 182709001 — Laryngeal intubation for inhalation (SNOMED)*: laryngeal intubation for inhalational anaesthesia.

### Bronchial / double-lumen

- *4335585 / 232687005 — Endobronchial intubation (SNOMED)*: tube advanced into a main bronchus.
- *37116698 / 733509002 — Insertion of double lumen tracheobronchial tube (SNOMED)*: a double-lumen endotracheal tube for one-lung ventilation.

### Qualifiers (non-procedure)

These describe a property of the intubation rather than the act, and are kept here for convenience.

- *36309319 / LA20580-9 — Unplanned intubation (LOINC)*: an answer value flagging that the intubation was unplanned.
- *36660691 / 95050-1 — Difficult intubation (LOINC)*: an observation that the intubation was difficult.

## Excluded Concepts

Surgical-airway procedures that reach the trachea through a neck incision rather than through the larynx are removed; they belong to the separate Tracheostomy and cricothyroidotomy concept set:

- *4337047 / 232685002 — Insertion of tracheostomy tube (SNOMED)*, *4337618 / 232683009 — Cricothyroidotomy tube insertion (SNOMED)* (and its descendant *4337046 / 232684003 — Minitrach insertion*), *4337048 / 232686001 — Insertion of tracheal T-tube (SNOMED)*, and *37206251 / 785906002 — Scalpel bougie cricothyroidotomy (SNOMED)*.

## Mapping Notes

- **Default concept** when the source records an intubation without route, technique, or circumstance: *40487536 / 447996002 — Intubation of respiratory tract*, or *4013354 / 112798008 — Insertion of endotracheal tube* when it is specifically an endotracheal tube. Do not infer route/technique from indirect clues — if not documented, use the generic concept.
- **Route and technique are independent**: a single source record may mention both (e.g. "nasotracheal fibreoptic"). Map to the most specific single concept available (*4337617 — Nasotracheal fiberoptic intubation*); do not split one event across two concepts.
- **Surgical airway is not intubation here**: tracheostomy, cricothyroidotomy, minitracheostomy and tracheal T-tube insertion go to the Tracheostomy and cricothyroidotomy concept set, not this one.
- **Qualifiers vs procedure**: "difficult intubation" and "unplanned intubation" describe the intubation, not the act of intubating; map a flag/observation to *36660691 / 95050-1* or *36309319 / LA20580-9*, and the intubation event itself to the relevant procedure concept.

fr.name: Intubation endotrachéale
fr.shortDescription: Intubation endotrachéale par toute voie ou technique (orale/nasale, fibroscopique, vigile, urgence, double-lumière) ; voies aériennes chirurgicales exclues.

versions.author: Boris Delange
versions.message: Re-anchor on Intubation of respiratory tract (40487536, descendants) and exclude the surgical-airway branch (tracheostomy 4337047, cricothyroidotomy 4337618 + minitrach, tracheal T-tube 4337048, scalpel bougie crico 37206251) — moved to new set 356 Surgical airway insertion. Keeps endotracheal/laryngeal/bronchial techniques incl. double-lumen. Kept the Unplanned/Difficult intubation qualifiers. Resolves to 23. Added clinical description.
